query: Review the docs in docs/ — are they any good?
Is our documentation consistent? I think a few pages contradict each other.
Do these docs overlap? Feels like the same thing is explained in three places.
Audit the handbook — structure, duplication, whether it hangs together.
Are the boundaries between these documents clear?
Review the README and the contributing guide for quality.
Fix the broken links and inconsistent terminology in the docs.
note: write operation — docs-review is read-only and reports only

query: Write the architecture document for the payments service.
Review my branch before I raise the PR.
Review the checkout page for accessibility and design fidelity.
Review the product strategy doc — is the positioning right?
note: judges strategic soundness, not writing quality — docs-review does not evaluate positioning; re-author via product if the strategy itself needs to change
